Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Sky blue background (node 0) fills most of the 64x64 board - White robot/character shape (node 2, 135 px) at upper-left area (rows ~11-25, cols ~11-25) with two red 3x3 "feet" blocks (nodes 3, 4) below it - Green rectangular target (node 5, 144 px) at lower-right (rows 29-49, cols 41-49) with a sky blue hollow center (node 6) - Two red 3x3 blocks (nodes 7, 8) positioned to the left of the green target - Charcoal bar (node 1) at top edge - likely HUD/timer
- Goal model: Move the white character to the green target area.
- Action model: UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT likely move the character. Need to test.
- Plan: Try moving RIGHT to see if the character moves, then navigate toward the green target.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The RIGHT action was executed and the board changed. Let me check what changed by comparing the before and after frames. I need to see if the white character moved or if something else changed.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (updated):
- RIGHT action caused a board change. Need to verify what moved.
- White character may have shifted right. The step counter increased from 0 to 1.
- Goal: Move white character to green target.
- Plan: Check what changed, then continue navigating toward the green target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

# Find the white node in both frames
before_white = [n for n in before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W'][0]
after_white = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W'][0]

print("Before white boundary:", before_white['boundary'][:8])
print("After white boundary:", after_white['boundary'][:8])
print("Before white pixels:", before_white['pixels'])
print("After white pixels:", after_white['pixels'])
print("Before white hash:", before_white['hash'])
print("After white hash:", after_white['hash'])

# Check red blocks too
for c in ['R', 'N']:
    before_nodes = [n for n in before['nodes'] if n['color'] == c]
    after_nodes = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == c]
    print(f"\n{c} before: {len(before_nodes)}, after: {len(after_nodes)}")
    for bn, an in zip(before_nodes, after_nodes):
        print(f"  {c}: before boundary={bn['boundary'][:4]}, after boundary={an['boundary'][:4]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white character and its two red feet moved right by 3 columns. The other red blocks (near the green target) and the green target stayed in place. So RIGHT moves the character right by 3 cells.
Now I need to figure out the goal. The character is at cols ~14-28 (after moving), and the green target is at cols 41-49. I need to move right and down to reach the target.
Let me calculate: the character needs to go from roughly row 11-25 to row 29-49 (down) and from col 14-28 to col 41-49 (right).
Let me try to navigate efficiently. Each RIGHT moves 3 cols, each DOWN moves 3 rows (likely). Let me test DOWN first.

sections.content:
World model (updated):
- RIGHT moves the white character + red feet right by 3 columns (same shape, hash unchanged)
- Red blocks near green target and green target itself don't move
- Character started at cols 11-25, now at cols 14-28
- Green target is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49
- Goal: Navigate character to the green target
